benchmark_case_name: Human-Low AI-High Conflict (Runtime-Generated Weak-Artifact Reopen Family)
summary: The benchmark now has a complementary runtime-generated verdict showing that a human-facing weak-artifact rejection can remain attached to the reopen chain instead of being flattened into a generic disagreement record.
notes: This verdict extends RV-002 beyond a generic reopen trace by binding the reopened runtime path back to an explicit weak-artifact rejection family.
The remaining gap is broader source diversity: additional runtime-generated human disconfirmation families should come from more than one artifact and more than one reopen source.
